title: hex_digit helper function silently returns 0 for invalid characters
description:
File: rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/gatt.rs:74-80

The hex_digit const function returns 0 for invalid hex characters instead of signaling an error:

```rust
const fn hex_digit(c: u8) -> u8 {
    match c {
        b'0'..=b'9' => c - b'0',
        b'a'..=b'f' => c - b'a' + 10,
        b'A'..=b'F' => c - b'A' + 10,
        _ => 0,  // Silent failure!
    }
}
```

While this is only used with hardcoded UUID strings at compile time (making runtime impact unlikely), silent failures violate fail-fast principles and could mask typos in UUID constants.

IMPACT: Low - only affects compile-time UUID parsing with hardcoded strings.

FIX: Use panic! in the catch-all arm since this is a const fn and will produce a compile-time error if invalid characters are present:
```rust
_ => panic!("invalid hex digit")
```
